name: Nicotinamide Mononucleotide
summary: NMN is an NAD+ precursor studied for metabolic and aging-related biomarkers. Human evidence is emerging, with limited clinical outcome data and uncertain long-term safety.
description: NAD+ precursor with early human evidence for modest insulin-sensitivity or biomarker effects; clinical endpoints remain unproven.
safetyNotes: GI upset and headache possible; long-term human safety data remain limited.
contraindications: active or undiagnosed cancer history warrants oncologist guidance before use due to theoretical support of cellular energy metabolism in tumor growth
diabetes medication such as insulin or metformin may need monitoring due to NMN's influence on blood sugar regulation
antihypertensive medication may have an additive blood pressure lowering effect
pregnancy or breastfeeding without clinician guidance due to insufficient safety data
